definitions: To direct your eyes towards something or someone in order to see them.
To search for something; to try to find it.
To appear or seem to be a certain way.
examples: Look at the beautiful sunset over the sea.
You look tired today; did you sleep well?

definitions: An act of directing your eyes to see something; a glance.
The appearance or expression of a person or thing.
examples: Take a look at this photo I took yesterday.
He had a worried look on his face.

definitions: To put your hand or another part of your body onto something.
To be or come so close to something that there is no space between.
examples: Please do not touch the paintings in the museum.
Their hands touched as they reached for the same cup.

definitions: The sense that lets you feel things through your skin.
A small amount of something.
examples: She felt the soft touch of the blanket.
Add a touch of salt to the soup.

definitions: Fixed or held firmly in place; not loose.
Fitting very closely to the body, sometimes too closely.
Stretched or pulled so as not to be slack.
examples: Make sure the lid is tight so the water doesn't spill.
These shoes are too tight for me.

definitions: Material made of threads or cords knotted into a mesh, used for catching fish or dividing a sports court.
An amount of money remaining after all costs and deductions have been taken away.
examples: The fishermen pulled the heavy net out of the water.
The ball hit the net and bounced back.

definitions: A distinct stage or period in a process of change or development.
One part of a larger plan that happens in a set order.
examples: The first phase of the project is now complete.
Children go through many phases as they grow up.

definitions: In some religions, a place of suffering and punishment after death.
A very unpleasant or difficult situation or experience.
examples: The long traffic jam in the heat was absolute hell.
Many religions describe both heaven and hell.

definitions: Used in the phrase 'on behalf of' to mean as a representative of, or for the benefit of, someone.
examples: She accepted the award on behalf of the whole team.
I am writing on behalf of my client.

definitions: A measured amount of a medicine to be taken at one time.
An amount of something experienced at one time.
examples: Take one dose of the syrup twice a day.
He needs a healthy dose of common sense.

definitions: The crime of forcing another person to have sexual intercourse without their consent.
examples: Rape is a serious crime that is punished by law.
The charity supports survivors of rape and abuse.

definitions: A title used before the surname or full name of a married woman.
examples: Mrs. Perera teaches English at our school.
Good morning, Mrs. Silva.

definitions: The annual Christian festival celebrating the birth of Jesus Christ, held on 25 December.
The period of days around 25 December when this is celebrated.
examples: We give presents to each other at Christmas.
The whole family comes home for Christmas.

definitions: The first month of the year, after December and before February.
examples: The new year begins in January.
Her birthday is on the tenth of January.

definitions: The second month of the year, after January and before March.
examples: February is the shortest month of the year.
We are getting married in February.

definitions: The fourth month of the year, after March and before May.
examples: It often rains in April.
The school term starts in April.

definitions: The sixth month of the year, after May and before July.
examples: Schools close for the holidays in June.
We went to the beach last June.

definitions: The seventh month of the year, after June and before August.
examples: The weather is very hot in July.
His exam is in July.

definitions: The ninth month of the year, after August and before October.
examples: The new school year begins in September.
We will visit you in September.

definitions: The tenth month of the year, after September and before November.
examples: The leaves turn brown in October.
Her wedding is in October.

definitions: The eleventh month of the year, after October and before December.
examples: It gets colder in November.
He was born in November.

definitions: The twelfth and last month of the year, after November.
examples: Christmas is in December.
The year ends on the last day of December.

definitions: The day of the week between Sunday and Tuesday, usually the first working day.
examples: School starts again on Monday.
I have a meeting every Monday morning.

definitions: The day of the week between Monday and Wednesday.
examples: The shop is closed on Tuesday.
We will meet next Tuesday.

definitions: The day of the week between Tuesday and Thursday.
examples: Wednesday is the middle of the working week.
Her dance class is on Wednesday.

definitions: The day of the week between Wednesday and Friday.
examples: We have a test on Thursday.
He arrives on Thursday evening.

definitions: The day of the week between Thursday and Saturday, the last working day of the week.
examples: Everyone is happy when Friday comes.
We are going out on Friday night.

definitions: The day of the week between Friday and Sunday, part of the weekend.
examples: I do my shopping on Saturday.
The children play football every Saturday.

definitions: The day of the week between Saturday and Monday, a day of rest for many people.
examples: We have a big lunch with family on Sunday.
The shops open late on Sunday.
